L1.area: Data value parsing and input processing pipeline

L2.description: An arbitrary code execution vulnerability exists where user-controlled string values in the JSON data are automatically interpreted and executed as code without proper sanitization. When displaying or editing data from untrusted sources, crafted input values can trigger execution of arbitrary JavaScript in the browser, leading to full compromise of the client-side application.

L3.area: Data value parsing and input processing pipeline
L3.description: An arbitrary code execution vulnerability exists where user-controlled string values in the JSON data are automatically interpreted and executed as code without proper sanitization. When displaying or editing data from untrusted sources, crafted input values can trigger execution of arbitrary JavaScript in the browser, leading to full compromise of the client-side application.

ground_truth.reason: The default value parser uses eval() to execute any string beginning with 'function' as JavaScript code, enabling arbitrary code execution when processing untrusted JSON data.
